target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-29 — head `9f4b13fff10`: fix the `host_write_allow` audit-detail overstatement (ClawSweeper [P1]/[P2]).** The finding interpolated the real allowlisted subset (`writeToolsInAllow`) in its opening clause but then hardcoded "the `write` and `edit` coding tools" as exposed — so a subset config (`hostFsWrite: true` + `gateway.tools.allow: ["edit"]`) was wrongly told the sibling `write` primitive is also reachable. Now the exposure clause names only the allowlisted write-class tool(s) (`src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts`). Added a regression test asserting the `edit`-only detail names `edit` and **not** `write`. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container — red/green: against the prior code the new test fails (`expected … not to contain \`write\``); with the fix it passes. Full gate green: audit + `tools-invoke-http` 176/176 + `schema.help.quality` 23/23, `tsgo:core` clean, oxlint clean. The only remaining item is maintainer security/product acceptance of the host-FS direct-invoke boundary.

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `448d4813e8f`: fix the plugin-name compatibility on the REAL plugin path (addresses ClawSweeper [P1]).** The previous fix preserved a same-named plugin only at the final gateway deny filter, but the production plugin loader filters `pluginToolDenylist` names first — so a same-named plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` was still dropped before that filter (the bot was right; the prior unit proof passed only because the mocked `createOpenClawTools` ignored the denylist). Fix: in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`, the host-FS coding names are now excluded from the `pluginToolDenylist` handed to the loader **when they are denied only by the host-FS default** (explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / owner-only still deny the plugin); the built-in stays gated at the final filter. The test mock now **mirrors the production loader** (drops a plugin-owned tool whose name is in `pluginToolDenylist`), so the regression tests exercise the real path. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container — **red/green**: reverting the exclusion makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`; with the fix they pass. The real resolver output was confirmed directly (the `pluginToolDenylist` it passes excludes `read`/`write`/`edit` in the host-FS-default case, includes a name under explicit `gateway.tools.deny`). Full gate green: `tools-invoke-http` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19/19, `schema.help.quality` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, **`tsgo:core` typecheck clean** (also fixed pre-existing `defaultGatewayDeny`/`ownerOnlyGatewayDeny` `.includes(string)` type errors my earlier `tsc` check had missed), oxlint clean.

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `228ade8544e`: rebased the full stack onto current `main`.** Conflict resolved (`scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` surface budgets reconciled to main's current values + this stack's deltas; `config-types` deprecated budget 416→417 for the new public `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type). The plugin-compat [P1] fix (source-aware host-FS default-deny) survives the rebase and is **re-proven against current main**: in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container, reverting only the resolver fix makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`, and restoring it passes — so the regression exists on current main and the fix removes it. Full gate green on the rebased head: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, oxlint clean, typecheck clean. Now `MERGEABLE` against main. The host-FS write boundary still needs explicit maintainer/security acceptance (a product decision, not an author fix).

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `acb9ab0a39d`: fix ClawSweeper [P1] plugin-name compatibility.** Adding `read`/`write`/`edit` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` name-denied any *same-named plugin tool* over `/tools/invoke` + SDK `tools.invoke`, so a pre-existing non-optional plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` returned 404 even with the host-FS opt-ins off. Per the resolver, the default-deny is now **source-aware** (`src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` `HOST_FS_BUILTIN_CODING_DENY_NAMES` + the gateway deny filter in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): a same-named tool whose *only* deny reason is the host-FS built-in coding default is preserved when it carries plugin metadata (`getPluginToolMeta`); the built-in coding tool (no plugin meta) stays gated behind the owner gate + opt-in, and owner-only / explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / `exec`/`fs_write`/etc. are unaffected. Two regression tests assert a plugin `read`/`write` stays reachable **without** a new `gateway.tools.allow` entry. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23; oxlint clean. Red/green proof: reverting only the resolver fix makes both new tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`. **Still pending: rebase onto current `main`** (main refactored the gateway deny-handling path, so the rebase needs careful re-expression — tracked separately).

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `61e6f29bed` (config-help owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]/[P3]).** Restacked onto #85664's new base `7f9876c2b2`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) and `pnpm check:docs` both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `661d6838c0` (docs owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]).** Restacked onto #85664's docs-updated read base `ca46eb9946`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `afe36ccf45` is **docs**: the `read` + `write`/`edit` truth tables in `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` and the `hostFsRead`/`hostFsWrite` bullets in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces — directly resolving ClawSweeper's **[P2]** ("Document the owner gate for file-tool opt-ins"). No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).

---

Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the `resolveGatewayScopedTools` resolver). Same **triple-key owner-gated** shape as #85664's `read`: a surface-specific class opt-in **plus** the per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` **plus** `senderIsOwner === true`, all on the `http` surface.

## Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base (head `afe36ccf456f`, base upstream `023ce6e96c38`)

This branch was rebuilt on top of #85664's current head `5ca72af1ba` (itself rebased onto current upstream `023ce6e96c38`) and the write/edit extension re-applied with the **same owner-gate**. This directly resolves the prior ClawSweeper [P1].

**Bot [P1] (resolved):** upstream commit `02182d5a3031` ("refactor: remove sender owner tool gating") deleted the `ownerOnly` enforcement helper, so the earlier `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag this PR added became dead code — a trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller could have reached an allowlisted mutating tool with no owner check. **Fix:** this PR no longer relies on that tag. `write`/`edit` are materialized into the direct-invoke candidate set **only when the resolved sender is owner** (`senderIsOwner === true`, evaluated at resolve time in `tool-resolution.ts`), exactly mirroring #85664's read gate. A trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller is now refused even with both config keys set (proven below, cases 2 & 4).

## Triple-key gating

To reach a write-class tool on the direct-invoke surface, ALL must hold:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` includes the tool name | Per-tool enablement. | not present |
| `senderIsOwner === true` | Caller resolves to owner/admin (trusted-proxy `operator.admin`, or shared-secret bearer). | — |

Truth table (tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`, surface = `http`):

| `allow` ∋ `T` | `hostFsWrite` | sender owner | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|---|
| no  | no  | —   | ❌ |
| yes | no  | yes | ❌ (class opt-in off; built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes | yes | ❌ (not in allow) |
| yes | yes | **no**  | ❌ (**owner gate** — refused) |
| yes | yes | yes | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (behind `hostFsWrite: true` + allow + owner).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` + allow + owner.
- **`apply_patch`:** present in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for defense-in-depth, but the coding-tools factory produces no `apply_patch` entry, so it is never materialized (proven below, case 7).
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class; out of scope for this PR.

## Audit

- `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` fires only when BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` AND a write-class name is in `allow` (mirrors `host_read_allow`); escalates to `critical` on non-loopback bind.
- `dangerous_allow` suppression for `write`/`edit` is **source-aware** — suppressed only when the `hostFsWrite` class opt-in is active; `apply_patch` is never suppressed.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — `hostFsWrite` schema entry
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — field help + label
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — owner-gated write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win plugin name collision when class opt-in active
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — write coding-tool collision precedence (owner→built-in, non-owner→plugin, opt-in-off→plugin)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — `host_write_allow` dual-key + source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression cases (incl. `apply_patch` never suppressed)
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — coding-tool factory plumbing
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`, `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — `hostFsWrite` opt-in, triple-key truth tables, `host_write_allow` catalog row

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's owner-gated direct-invoke `read` to also expose `write` and `edit` over the Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke` surfaces, behind the same triple key (class opt-in `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` + per-tool `gateway.tools.allow` + `senderIsOwner`). Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base so write/edit materialize only for an owner sender — resolving the prior [P1] where upstream `02182d5a3031` made the old `ownerOnly` tag dead code.

**Real environment tested:** Built fresh from head `afe36ccf456f` inside a `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (full runtime build → `dist/index.js`; SDK → `packages/sdk/dist/index.mjs`). Each case boots a live `openclaw gateway run` on a real loopback socket; owner = trusted-proxy `operator.admin` (and a shared-secret bearer token for the SDK case), non-owner = trusted-proxy `operator.write`. Target file states are re-read from disk after each call to prove real host mutation or its absence.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `PROOF_HEAD=afe36ccf456f bash .proof-63919.sh` inside the container — boots nine gateway configs and dispatches `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` over HTTP `/tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke`, asserting the HTTP status and re-reading each target file from disk.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output captured from the container at head `afe36ccf456f`:

```
=== HEAD: afe36ccf456f ===

### LIVE PROOF (HTTP trusted-proxy surface) ###

--- 1 W-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 24 bytes to /work/.proof63/w1.txt"}]}}
  file=OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f
--- 2 W-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=PRESEED
--- 3 E-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /work/.proof63/e3.txt."}],"details":{"diff":" 1 ALPHA\\n-2 BETA\\n+2 GAMMA-afe36ccf456f", ...}}}
  file=ALPHA / GAMMA-afe36ccf456f
--- 4 E-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  file=ALPHA / BETA  (unchanged)
--- 5 W-DENIED-no-opt-in (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=false, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 6 W-DENIED-no-allow (allow=[] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 7 APPLYPATCH-denied (allow=[apply_patch] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=apply_patch) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
--- 8 READ-REGRESSION (allow=[read] hostFsRead=true, operator.admin, tool=read) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"READ-BASE-OK-afe36ccf456f\\n"}]}}

### LIVE PROOF (SDK token surface — shared-secret bearer = owner) ###

--- 9 SDK-W-OWNER (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, token bearer, tool=write) ---
  [SDK token] {"ok":true,"toolName":"write","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 28 bytes to /work/.proof63/w9.txt"}]},"source":"core"}
  file=SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=afe36ccf456f ===
```

Independent re-read of persisted file states after the run: `w1.txt`=`OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`, `w2.txt`=`PRESEED` (non-owner write left it untouched), `e3.txt`=`ALPHA/GAMMA-afe36ccf456f`, `e4.txt`=`ALPHA/BETA` (non-owner edit left it untouched), `w5.txt` and `w6.txt` absent (never created), `w9.txt`=`SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`.

**Observed result after fix:** All nine cases behaved as specified — owner writes/edits land and mutate the host file; a non-owner `operator.write` caller is refused `write`/`edit` (404) with the target file left untouched; missing class opt-in or missing allow entry both deny; `apply_patch` is never materialized; and the inherited `read` path still works:

| # | Case | Config | Caller | HTTP/SDK | File state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | write owner | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 wrote 24 bytes | written |
| 2 | write **non-owner** | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 3 | edit owner | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 replaced 1 block | edited |
| 4 | edit **non-owner** | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 5 | write no opt-in | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=false | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 6 | write no allow | allow=[], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 7 | apply_patch | allow=[apply_patch], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | factory never materializes it |
| 8 | read regression | allow=[read], hostFsRead=true | `operator.admin` | 200 + contents | #85664 base intact |
| 9 | write owner (SDK) | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | token bearer | ok=true wrote 28 bytes | written |

target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where sandbox-enabled agents could create a media file in their workspace, then fail to send it through QQBot when the reply used the sandbox-visible `/workspace/...` path.

This affects the QQBot media delivery workflow for sandboxed agent replies, including `<qqmedia>` block replies, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payloads, and host-read media loading.

## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for first-pass review. The current update has fresh QQBot/Core regression tests, type checking, near-real gateway/message-delivery proof for the `/workspace/...` path, a redacted live QQBot C2C media-tag proof, and a passing ClawSweeper commit review.
- Root cause: sandboxed agents see generated files under the virtual root `/workspace`, but the remaining QQBot media paths still treated `/workspace/...` as an absolute host path instead of resolving that virtual root through the scoped agent workspace. That left gateway `<qqmedia>` block replies, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media paths, and host-read media loading out of sync with the scoped workspace contract.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the fix maps only the sandbox virtual root `/workspace` to the already configured `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` before local media validation or host-read loading. This repairs the source invariant shared by the affected QQBot media entry points: sandbox-visible workspace paths must resolve to the same scoped host workspace that the session authorized.
- Patch quality notes: the additional host-read error handling and `expect.any(...)` assertions are inherited from the existing PR diff and are covered by the focused tests. This update does not add a new fallback root or bypass validation; it preserves the existing scoped local-root checks, adds a traversal regression for `/workspace/../outside-report.docx`, and now preserves already-authorized host `/workspace/...` paths before falling back to sandbox virtual-root mapping. Maintainer feedback on QQBot media skill wording is addressed by making `<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>` the generic guidance and documenting `/workspace/...` only as a sandbox-visible path source.

## Linked context

- Related: #92827
- PR rescue target: #92872
- Behavior or issue addressed: sandbox-enabled agents can generate media files in their workspace and send them through QQBot using `/workspace/...` paths in block replies or structured media payloads.
- Why it matters / User impact: users can ask a sandboxed agent to create a document, image, voice file, video, or other supported file and send it through QQBot without the file being rejected only because the agent referenced the sandbox path it can see.
- What did NOT change: this does not add new QQBot upload roots, does not relax scoped media roots, does not change file-size limits, does not add a new public API, and does not claim live QQ group delivery coverage. The live external proof used QQBot C2C because that was the available authorized session.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the source of truth remains the session-scoped `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, `mediaAccess.localRoots`, scoped media roots, and OpenClaw media directory. `/workspace` is treated as a virtual alias for the current agent workspace only; all non-workspace absolute paths keep existing behavior.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: this is an update to the existing linked PR for #92827, not a competing PR. The change stays inside the current QQBot sandbox media contract already under review in #92872.
- Out of scope: no schema/config migration, no new provider/channel contract, no permission broadening for arbitrary host paths, no live external QQ group proof, and no unrelated channel changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Validation `qqbot-gateway-media-focused-postcommit` passed on the submitted commit. A separate redacted live QQBot C2C proof also sent the sandbox-generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt` through the high-level `<qqmedia>` tag path.

  ```text
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-messaging.config.ts

   RUN  v4.1.8 [local path redacted]


   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  41 passed (41)
     Start at  12:27:59
     Duration  13.00s (transform 8.85s, setup 841ms, import 15.77s, tests 292ms, environment 0ms)

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.75s
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  - Gateway `<qqmedia>/workspace/sandbox-report.docx</qqmedia>` block replies resolve to the configured agent workspace and reach the QQBot media sender with a scoped local path.
  - Gateway `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payloads with `path: "/workspace/payload-workspace-report.pdf"` resolve to the configured agent workspace and reach the QQBot media sender with a scoped local path.
  - Host-read media loading maps `/workspace/report.docx` to the real `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` path before calling `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`, so sandbox read callbacks receive the host workspace path instead of the virtual path.
  - Already-authorized host paths that literally live under `/workspace/...` are preserved before sandbox virtual-root remapping is tried, so an attachment root such as `/workspace/attachments` is not silently rewritten into another agent workspace.
  - Relative media paths are resolved only against the scoped agent workspace, not against the process current working directory.
  - Escape attempts such as `/workspace/../outside-report.docx` remain blocked and do not call the QQBot media sender.
  - Live QQBot C2C proof: a real sandbox-enabled agent generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt` (49 bytes, content marker `PR 92872 QQBot sandbox workspace media live proof`). Sending `<qqmedia>/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt</qqmedia>` through the QQBot high-level tag path returned QQ API `200 OK` for upload and send, with a QQBot message id returned. App id, secret, provider key, token, full sender id, and full message ids are redacted.
- What was not tested: no live external QQ group delivery was exercised in this environment because the available authorized external QQBot session was C2C. The proof combines local near-real gateway/message-delivery behavior, host-read behavior, and redacted live QQBot C2C media-tag delivery on the submitted diff.

## Tests and validation

- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: a gateway block reply with `<qqmedia>/workspace/sandbox-report.docx</qqmedia>` resolves through the configured agent workspace and reaches the QQBot sender.
  - Scenario locked in: a gateway block reply with `<qqmedia>/workspace/../outside-report.docx</qqmedia>` remains blocked by scoped local-root validation.
  - Scenario locked in: a structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payload with `/workspace/...` resolves through the configured agent workspace and reaches the QQBot sender.
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: host-read media loading receives the mapped host workspace path for `/workspace/report.docx` before calling `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`.
  - Scenario locked in: an already-authorized host path under `/workspace/attachments` is preserved before virtual workspace mapping is attempted.
  - Scenario locked in: relative outbound media paths are resolved only against `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`.
  - Scenario locked in: relative voice host-read paths still resolve inside `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` before staging the QQBot voice upload.
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` local file paths preserve already-authorized host `/workspace/...` roots before virtual workspace mapping.
  - Scenario locked in: relative structured payload paths are resolved only against `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the tests exercise the same QQBot gateway, structured-payload, and host-read boundaries that previously rejected or rewrote sandbox media paths, while keeping traversal behavior covered at the security boundary.

Validation after the previous submitted commit and this audit update:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  41 passed (41)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  7 passed (7)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  65 passed (65)
  ```

- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`

  ```text
  $ pnpm tsgo:test
  $ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  ```

Additional validation after the path-order audit update:

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/reply-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=dot`

  ```text
  Test Files  3 passed (3)
       Tests  46 passed (46)
  ```

- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`

  ```text
  passed
  ```

Validation for the maintainer-requested QQBot media skill wording update:

- `python -c 'from pathlib import Path; text=Path("extensions/qqbot/skills/qqbot-media/SKILL.md").read_text(); assert "<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>" in text; assert "<qqmedia>/workspace/文件名或URL</qqmedia>" not in text; assert "只有当沙箱 workspace-write 创建结果实际显示 `/workspace/...`" in text; assert "附件路径" in text; assert "URL 可以直接发送" in text; assert "QQBot 媒体目录" in text; print("skill wording ok")'`

  ```text
  skill wording ok
  ```

## Risk checklist

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: message-delivery`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus compatibility/availability/session-state signals from the QQBot outbound media path.
- Risk explanation: QQBot media delivery sits on a security-sensitive boundary because it turns an agent reply path into host file reads and outbound uploads. Mapping `/workspace` incorrectly could either keep breaking sandbox sends or accidentally allow paths outside the session workspace.
- Why acceptable: already-authorized absolute paths are checked first, sandbox virtual-root mapping is limited to the literal `/workspace` root as a fallback, and the existing scoped local-root checks still decide whether the resulting host path is allowed. The regression suite covers the allowed workspace case, the already-authorized host `/workspace/...` case, relative path resolution, and the rejected traversal case; the fix does not add a new trusted root.
- Compatibility/default behavior: relative paths continue to resolve inside the scoped workspace; ordinary absolute host paths keep existing behavior, including literal `/workspace/...` host paths that are already authorized by scoped roots; remote URLs and data sources continue through the existing URL/data handling.
- Large diff note: the overall PR is already larger because it previously threaded scoped media access across QQBot outbound paths. This update is the narrow follow-up requested by review: `/workspace` mapping, gateway/host-read regressions, and skill wording alignment.

## Current review state

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 `status: ⏳ waiting on author`: addressed by this update path, but the label should be removed only by the normal remote review/automation after submit. I did not manually edit labels.
- RF-002 Map `/workspace/...` media references and add gateway `<qqmedia>` regression: fixed. Added gateway block reply coverage for `/workspace/sandbox-report.docx` resolving to the configured agent workspace.
- RF-003 Security-boundary advisory for localRoots/readFile after `/workspace` mapping: disclosed and covered. `/workspace/...` maps to `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, while `/workspace/../outside-report.docx` remains blocked by scoped local-root validation.
- RF-004 Live proof gap for sandbox-generated `/workspace/...` QQBot media or QQ group send: addressed for live QQBot C2C media delivery and local near-real gateway/message-delivery coverage. A real sandbox-enabled agent generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt`; the same `/workspace/...` path was sent through the high-level `<qqmedia>` QQBot tag path and returned QQ API `200 OK` for upload and send. Live QQ group proof is explicitly not claimed because the available authorized external QQBot session was C2C.
- RF-005 Narrow repair for remaining absolute sandbox workspace path and stale QQBot media skill wording: fixed. The QQBot skill now presents `<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>` as the generic format; `/workspace/...` is documented only as a possible path an agent may actually see in sandbox workspace-write runs. Attachment-context paths and URLs are kept direct, and local paths remain limited to authorized scoped roots.
- RF-006 Map `/workspace` media tags through the scoped workspace in `outbound-media-send.ts`: fixed. Host-read paths are mapped before `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`, including the daily-limit metadata path.
- RF-007 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-008 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-009 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-010 Run `corepack pnpm check:test-types`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-011 Maintainer feedback from `sliverp` on QQBot media skill wording: fixed. The skill no longer teaches `/workspace/...` as the universal media format; it uses `<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>` and explains how to choose the path source for sandbox-created files, attachment-context paths, direct URLs, and authorized local media roots.
- RF-012 Additional path-order audit finding: fixed. Literal host paths already authorized under `/workspace/...` are now validated before sandbox virtual-root remapping is attempted, while relative paths still resolve only inside the scoped agent workspace.

Commit review state:

- ClawSweeper commit review: passed on the final single-commit update; no blocking findings.

target.title: [codex] mirror proactive deliveries into session transcripts
target.body:
## Summary
- Mirror proactive cron announce/failure delivery into custom/main session transcripts with a stable `cron-announce-mirror:v1:*` idempotency key.
- Keep routed direct, legacy dm, group, and channel session transcripts out of cron mirrors, including provider-owned suffixes such as Telegram `:topic:` and Slack thread suffixes.
- Mirror heartbeat/digest delivery into the active/base chat transcript with a stable `heartbeat-delivery-mirror:v1:*` idempotency key so follow-up replies can see the proactive message that was actually sent.
- Add focused regression coverage for both delivery paths.

Supersedes #86140 with the same bug class handled in one PR.

## Verification
- Based on `origin/main` `5f934830d38272dbe5ed12b453430599378dd936`; PR head is `0a806a46410479c718e79884c8cb9148c84645b9`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/delivery.failure-notify.test.ts` (1 file, 13 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts` (1 file, 14 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cron/delivery.ts src/cron/delivery.failure-notify.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` (`core`, `coreTests`)
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` temp-state cron alias proof (`target_session_key=main`, `canonical_session_key=agent:ops:work`, `alias_mirror_ok=true`)
- Added heartbeat text+mediaUrls regression coverage: transcript mirrors preserve the delivered heartbeat text instead of resolving to the attachment filename.

## Changelog candidate
- Proactive delivery: mirror cron announce/failure notifications and heartbeat/digest sends into the destination transcript so follow-up replies keep the delivered message in context.

Note: the changelog line is intentionally kept out of this PR diff to avoid repeated `CHANGELOG.md` conflicts while `main` is moving quickly. It can be added at landing.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Proactive outbound deliveries that are visible to a user now append the delivered assistant text to the transcript that the next turn will read. Cron announce/failure delivery mirrors only into custom/main session keys and skips routed direct/dm/group/channel chat transcripts, including provider-owned suffixes such as Telegram topics. Heartbeat/digest delivery mirrors into the active/base chat session key, so a reply after a WhatsApp-style proactive digest has the sent digest in context.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout `/Users/dross/openclaw-cron-transcript-mirror`, branch `codex/proactive-delivery-transcript-mirrors`, head `0a806a46410479c718e79884c8cb9148c84645b9`, based on `origin/main` `5f934830d38272dbe5ed12b453430599378dd936`, with a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. The proof uses a local in-process outbound plugin named `proofline`, so no private external account, phone number, endpoint, or token is needed. It exercises `sendCronAnnouncePayloadStrict` and `runHeartbeatOnce` through `sendDurableMessageBatch`, outbound delivery, and transcript append.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` from the repo root with an inline harness that registered a local `proofline` outbound adapter, seeded the default session store with a custom cron session key, a Telegram topic-suffixed group session key, and a direct heartbeat session key, delivered cron and heartbeat messages, then read the resulting transcript files from the temporary state directory.

Evidence after fix:

```text
OPENCLAW_RUNTIME_PROOF proactive delivery transcript mirrors
state_dir=/var/folders/69/5f9v9rq9045bwk3w75cht7wm0000gn/T/openclaw-proactive-mirror-proof.VAikaS
store_path=/var/folders/69/5f9v9rq9045bwk3w75cht7wm0000gn/T/openclaw-proactive-mirror-proof.VAikaS/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
send_results=[{"to":"cron-recipient","text":"proof cron mirror text"},{"to":"topic-recipient","text":"proof topic mirror skip text"},{"to":"heartbeat-user","text":"proof heartbeat mirror text"}]
cron_custom_transcript={"exists":true,"lineCount":2,"lastRole":"assistant","lastText":"proof cron mirror text","lastIdempotencyKey":null}
topic_group_transcript={"exists":false,"lineCount":0,"lastRole":null,"lastText":null,"lastIdempotencyKey":null}
heartbeat_transcript={"exists":true,"lineCount":2,"lastRole":"assistant","lastText":"proof heartbeat mirror text","lastIdempotencyKey":null}
cron_mirror_ok=true
topic_group_mirror_skipped=true
heartbeat_mirror_ok=true
```

Observed result after fix: All three sends reached the local outbound adapter. The custom cron session transcript was created and ended with the mirrored assistant message. The Telegram topic-suffixed group session still delivered but did not create a transcript mirror. The direct heartbeat session transcript was created and ended with the mirrored heartbeat/digest assistant message.

What was not tested: I did not run a real WhatsApp, Telegram, or Slack network send from this branch. The motivating live WhatsApp evidence was captured separately on a private Dross setup: a heartbeat/digest message was visible in WhatsApp, the user replied immediately after it, and the active session transcript did not contain the digest before this patch. That before-fix evidence is privacy-redacted and not attached as a PR image.

target.title: feat: add tokenomics plugin for local-first LLM spend accounting
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw computes a per-call `costUsd` on `model.usage` diagnostics, but nothing persists or aggregates it: there is no durable record of spend over time and no view of how much was paid vs. run for free. Operators cannot answer "what did my agents cost this week, by model, and how much did free/local models save me?"

This PR adds a local-first plugin that turns OpenClaw's existing cost signal into a durable **ledger** and a **spend report** — without duplicating OpenClaw's price catalog.

## Summary

Adds **`tokenomics`**, a local-first plugin that gives OpenClaw a durable spend **ledger** and a **spend report** (period + by-model rollups, free-vs-paid split, avoided-spend / counterfactual headline).

It does **not** maintain its own price list. It **consumes OpenClaw's own per-call cost** (`costUsd` on `model.usage`, which OpenClaw already computes from its provider/model catalog) and adds the accounting layer on top — deliberately *consume-and-enhance*, not a duplicate catalog.

## How it works

- A startup service subscribes to internal `model.usage` diagnostics and appends one row (`ts_utc`, `provider`, `model`, `tokens_in`, `tokens_out`, `cost_usd`) to `<stateDir>/tokenomics/ledger.jsonl`.
- **Cost precedence:** host-reported `costUsd` is authoritative → otherwise a free classification yields `$0` → otherwise an optional local override catalog (`pricing.json`) estimates it. Unknown models stay `$0`, so cost is never invented.
- **Baseline derived from observed spend** by default (highest effective `$/Mtok` among paid models actually used), so no separate price catalog is required. `pricing.json` is only an optional override.
- Read-only report at `GET /api/diagnostics/tokenomics` (`?format=text|json`, `?since=&until=&gran=`).

## Design

- **Host-neutral and offline:** nothing leaves the machine; no pricing feed is fetched at runtime.
- **Single-user, local-first storage:** append-only JSONL with synchronous, tolerant reads (a half-written line is skipped and counted in a warning). SQLite with windowed reads is the documented path if a deployment outgrows it.
- **`pricing.json` treated as trusted input:** rejected if group/world-writable, oversized, non-regular, or unparseable.

## Evidence

- **78 colocated unit tests pass** (mapping, ledger persistence, pricing fallback + validation, derived baseline, inclusive date bounds, and the HTTP route incl. 400/405/503/HEAD).
- Green locally and in CI: extension tests, bundled type-aware lint, typecheck, format, knip, shrinkwrap, plus the repo packaging/lint-suppression registry tests.
- Sample `?format=text` report and `pricing.json` schema are documented in `extensions/tokenomics/README.md`.

## Evidence — live real-behavior proof

Per the automated review's request for install/enable/real-model-call/report-route proof, I built and ran this branch end-to-end on a clean Linux box (Node 22.23, pnpm 11.2.2, gateway bound to loopback) and exercised it with two **real** paid providers (Groq and MiniMax). Keys and the gateway token are redacted.

**1. The real blocker is a capability grant, not the plugin logic.** On unmodified `main`, `createServiceContext` (`src/plugins/services.ts`) grants `internalDiagnostics.onEvent` **only** to service ids `diagnostics-otel` / `diagnostics-prometheus`. With that gate, `tokenomics` logs:

```
[plugins] tokenomics: internal diagnostics capability unavailable; spend will not be recorded
```

…and records nothing. This matches the review finding exactly.

**2. With a one-line allowlist addition** (the maintainer decision below), the full chain works. The only change outside the extension:

```diff
   const isDiagnosticsExporter =
     params.service?.pluginId === params.service?.service.id &&
     (params.service?.service.id === "diagnostics-otel" ||
-      params.service?.service.id === "diagnostics-prometheus");
+      params.service?.service.id === "diagnostics-prometheus" ||
+      params.service?.service.id === "tokenomics");
```

**3. Plugin enabled + capability granted (gateway startup log):**

```
[gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: ... tokenomics; 0.3s)
[plugins] tokenomics: recording model spend to <stateDir>/tokenomics/ledger.jsonl
```

**4. Two real model calls ingested** (`openclaw agent` → real provider HTTP → `model.usage` with OpenClaw's authoritative `costUsd`), appended to `ledger.jsonl`:

```json
{"ts_utc":"2026-06-27T16:40:39.377Z","provider":"groq","model":"llama-3.3-70b-versatile","tokens_in":16445,"tokens_out":9,"cost_usd":0.00970966,"host":"openclaw"}
{"ts_utc":"2026-06-27T16:43:53.066Z","provider":"minimax","model":"MiniMax-M3","tokens_in":16056,"tokens_out":53,"cost_usd":0.00957648,"host":"openclaw"}
```

**5. The Gateway report route returns the aggregated report** — `GET /api/diagnostics/tokenomics` with gateway-token auth.

JSON:

```json
{
  "since": "2026-05-28", "until": "2026-06-27", "days": 30, "bucket_gran": "day",
  "by_model": [
    {"model":"llama-3.3-70b-versatile","cost_usd":0.00970966,"tokens":16454,"calls":1,"billed":true},
    {"model":"MiniMax-M3","cost_usd":0.00957648,"tokens":16109,"calls":1,"billed":true}
  ],
  "total_cost_usd": 0.01928614, "total_tokens": 32563, "total_calls": 2,
  "billed_tokens": 32563, "free_tokens": 0,
  "counterfactual_usd": 0.019358055635979893,
  "baseline_model": "MiniMax-M3", "baseline_usd_per_mtok": 0.5944801042895277
}
```

`?format=text`:

```
Tokenomics
2026-05-28 → 2026-06-27 (30d)

spent          $0.02  (2 calls, 32.6k tok)
avoided        $0.00  free tokens valued at baseline
counterfactual $0.02  all tokens @ MiniMax-M3 ($0.59/Mtok)

model                      calls     tokens     cost  tag
llama-3.3-70b-versatile        1      16.5k    $0.01  paid
MiniMax-M3                     1      16.1k    $0.01  paid
```

This confirms the plugin consumes OpenClaw's authoritative `costUsd`, persists it, and serves a correct period/by-model report with the derived baseline + counterfactual — end to end, on real provider traffic.

## Roadmap (follow-up PR)

A subscription/plan layer for billing models per-token pricing can't express:
- **Flat + session/rate limits** (e.g. OpenAI) and **flat + monthly token quota** (e.g. Minimax annual tiers).
- Reports subscription ROI vs metered-equivalent cost and quota utilization.
The foundation ledger already captures the `provider`/`model`/`tokens`/`cost_usd` primitives needed to layer this on.

## Note for maintainers — two asks

1. **Ingestion seam (the one real decision).** As shown above, the plugin's only hard dependency on core is the trusted internal-diagnostics subscription, which `main` currently restricts to the two diagnostics exporters. Please pick the direction you prefer and I'll finalize the PR to match:
   - **(a)** Add `tokenomics` to the trusted internal-diagnostics allowlist (the one-line change shown above), treating it as an approved official accounting consumer; or
   - **(b)** Point me at the canonical seam you'd rather it consume (e.g. `usage.cost` / the native usage-ledger tracked in #13219) and I'll **redesign ingestion + persistence onto that contract** instead of the diagnostics subscription.

2. **`dependency-guard`.** Fails because this PR adds a new workspace package to the dependency graph, which requires a maintainer/secops override (`/allow-dependencies-change`). All other checks are green. Happy to adjust packaging/storage (incl. SQLite-backed plugin state) to match your preference.

target.title: test(cli): add focused coverage for node CLI daemon and command registry
target.body:
## Summary

`src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` exposes seven branchy entry points (`runNodeDaemonInstall`, `runNodeDaemonUninstall`, `runNodeDaemonStart`, `runNodeDaemonRestart`, `runNodeDaemonStop`, `runNodeDaemonStatus`, plus `resolveNodeDefaults`), and `register.ts` wires them into the `openclaw node` Commander tree. On current main only one narrow registration smoke test exists (parses `node start --json`), and there is no `daemon.test.ts` at all. As called out in #83924 (test-gap triage), the install port validation, runtime gate, already-installed branch, TLS inheritance, status JSON shape, and runtime error paths were not exercised.

This PR adds focused colocated coverage that mirrors the existing patterns in `src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts` and `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts`. No source changes — purely test additions.

Closes #83924.

## Change Type

- [x] Test (no behavior change)

## Scope

- [x] CLI / Tests

## Linked Issue

- Closes #83924

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` had no colocated regression test on current main; `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts` only asserted the `node start --json` wrapper. The seven branches called out in #83924 (port validation, already-loaded short-circuit, runtime gate, TLS inheritance, JSON status, missing-unit hint, stopped-runtime hint) were uncovered, and the `node install/status/stop/restart/uninstall` command tree had no contract test.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `test/node-cli-daemon-coverage` rebased on upstream main commit `9ca52ce3d93071a0adc597e6c6808d141e58cee0`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` (Sweeper acceptance criteria, focused).
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts` (Sweeper acceptance criteria, full set).
3. Drift proof: temporarily removed the invalid-runtime guard from `runNodeDaemonInstall` and re-ran focused tests — the new `rejects an invalid --runtime value` case fails.
4. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
5. `pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts`
6. `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json` — clean.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts

 ✓  cli  ../../src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts (17 tests) 17ms
 ✓  cli  ../../src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts (10 tests) 4ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
```

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts

 ✓  cli       ../../src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts (20 tests) 160ms
 ✓  cli       ../../src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts (17 tests) 2ms
 ✓  cli       ../../src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts (10 tests) 4ms
 ✓  commands  ../../src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts (3 tests) 35ms

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
```

Drift proof (intentionally removed the invalid-runtime guard from `runNodeDaemonInstall`):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts

 ×  rejects an invalid --runtime value

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 16 passed (17)
```

```
$ pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
All matched files use the correct format.

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
(no diagnostics, exit 0)
```

**Observed result after fix**:

- New `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` (17 cases) covers all seven daemon entry points: `runNodeDaemonInstall` (invalid `--port`, invalid configured `node.gateway.port`, invalid `--runtime`, already-loaded short-circuit, force-reinstall, TLS inheritance, default 18789 port fallback, nix mode abort), `runNodeDaemonStart/Stop/Restart/Uninstall` (delegation contract to `lifecycle-core` with the `Node` service noun), and `runNodeDaemonStatus` (JSON snapshot+command+runtime payload, human-mode start hints, `readRuntime` rejection → `unknown` status, missing-unit error path, stopped-runtime error path).
- Extended `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts` by +5 cases for `node install/status/stop/restart/uninstall` option forwarding.
- Net: `node-cli` colocated tests grow from 5 → 27, with no source changes.

**What was not tested**: The launchd/systemd/Windows service runtime selection (`resolveNodeService`) is exercised through mocks rather than against real OS service managers — the existing daemon-cli integration tests cover that surface.

## Root Cause

Test gap. `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` was introduced in `c92ebd6a4104` together with one narrow registration smoke test, but the colocated daemon-level tests were never added. The Sweeper review (issue #83924) flagged the missing branch coverage with file-level pointers; this PR fills the gap.

## Regression Test Plan

- New `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` with 17 cases isolates each entry point through mocks for `node-daemon-install-helpers`, `node-daemon-runtime`, `node-host/config`, `daemon/node-service`, `daemon-cli/lifecycle-core`, `daemon-cli/response`, `daemon-cli/shared`, and `error-format`. The mocks mirror the patterns in `src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts`.
- 5 new cases in `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts` cover the Commander wiring for `install`, `status`, `stop`, `restart`, and `uninstall` so future flag drift is caught at the command layer.
- Drift proof: removing the invalid-runtime guard inside `runNodeDaemonInstall` makes the `rejects an invalid --runtime value` test fail, confirming the new tests guard the documented behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None — this PR is test-only. No changes to `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` or `register.ts`.

## Scope boundary

- Added: `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` (new), 5 new cases in `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts`, 1 CHANGELOG bullet under `### Tests`.
- No production source changes.
- No new test infrastructure or helpers — all mocks reuse `createCliRuntimeCapture` from `src/cli/test-runtime-capture.ts` and the standard vitest hoisted-mock pattern already used across `src/cli/daemon-cli`.

## Prior art

No prior PR found targeting #83924 at the time of this submission.

target.title: fix(codex): expose bound conversation dynamic tools
target.body:
## Summary
- expose OpenClaw dynamic plugin tools to native bound Codex conversation threads
- refresh managed Codex bindings when the dynamic-tool surface changes
- route Codex app-server dynamic tool calls back through OpenClaw plugin handlers with bounded timeouts
- keep bundled Codex host manifests from being shadowed by stale official global plugin installs

## Motivation
On macOS, the Kynver AgentOS OpenClaw plugin could be loaded and runtime inspection could see its `agent_os_*` contracts, but native Codex conversation threads did not receive those dynamic tool specs as callable tools. That meant `agent_os_get_context` and related tools were visible to OpenClaw inspection but missing from the Codex-visible tool surface.

This PR recovers the working local Track 2 patch from a macOS OpenClaw/Codex install where the AgentOS tools became callable after the Codex bridge change.

## Before / After
Before:
- plugin runtime inspection could see imported Kynver AgentOS tool contracts
- native bound Codex threads did not expose the imported dynamic tools to Codex tool calls

After:
- managed Codex conversation bindings include the current OpenClaw dynamic-tool surface
- tool-surface changes trigger binding refresh instead of leaving stale Codex-visible tools
- Codex dynamic tool calls dispatch through OpenClaw plugin handlers rather than failing as missing tools

## Verification
Focused tests run in the prepared recovery checkout:

```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
```

Result:
- plugin shard: 125 passed
- Codex extension shard: 19 passed

Additional checks:

```bash
git diff --cached --check -- .
```

The same whitespace check passed again after replaying the patch onto a fresh `openclaw/openclaw` clone. The fresh clone did not have dependencies installed, so the focused test command there stopped at missing `vitest/package.json` before tests started.

MacOS smoke from the patched local setup:
- fresh OpenClaw/Codex tool discovery exposed `agent_os_get_context`
- calling it reached the tool bridge successfully instead of failing as a missing tool

## Scope
The patch is limited to the Codex conversation binding / dynamic tool bridge and supporting plugin registry behavior needed for that path. No secrets or local machine paths are included.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add NEAR AI Cloud provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw does not currently expose NEAR AI Cloud as a bundled model provider.
- Solution: Adds a bundled `nearai` provider plugin for NEAR AI Cloud's OpenAI-compatible API at `https://cloud-api.near.ai/v1`.
- What changed: Adds API-key onboarding for `NEARAI_API_KEY`, dynamic public model catalog discovery with a static fallback, OpenAI-compatible replay/compat metadata, provider docs, generated plugin inventory entries, and focused tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No generic Gateway transport changes and no live provider use unless a user explicitly configures NEAR AI Cloud.
- Review note: AI-assisted.

## Motivation

- Users can configure NEAR AI Cloud TEE-backed inference through the same provider onboarding and model catalog surfaces as other bundled OpenAI-compatible providers.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: NEAR AI Cloud appears as a built-in provider and exposes its model catalog.
- Real environment tested: Linux source checkout using the project CLI.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw models list --all --provider nearai`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
nearai/anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5          text+image 195k        no    no
nearai/openai/gpt-5.5                      text       1025k       no    no
nearai/Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8            text       256k        no    no
nearai/zai-org/GLM-5.1-FP8                 text       198k        no    no
```

- Observed result after fix: The `nearai` provider resolves and lists NEAR AI Cloud model refs, including the default `nearai/zai-org/GLM-5.1-FP8`.
- What was not tested: Live inference, because `NEARAI_API_KEY` was not available in the test environment.
- Before evidence optional: Previous versions do not expose a bundled nearai provider, so the post-patch CLI output above is the after-fix proof for the newly added provider.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Adds `NEAR AI Cloud` / `nearai` as a built-in provider option.
- Adds `NEARAI_API_KEY`, `--auth-choice nearai-api-key`, and `--nearai-api-key <key>` setup paths.
- Adds provider documentation at `/providers/nearai`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This adds an optional outbound model provider that uses the existing provider auth path for `NEARAI_API_KEY`. Runtime catalog refresh is pinned to `cloud-api.near.ai` through the existing SSRF guard, and inference only occurs when the user chooses/configures the provider.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local source checkout with project scripts
- Model/provider: `nearai`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): No live NEAR AI API key configured

### Steps

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/nearai`
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/registry.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/provider-family-plugin-tests.test.ts`
3. `node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check`
4. `pnpm docs:list`
5. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
6. `pnpm plugins:sync:check`
7. `pnpm openclaw models list --all --provider nearai`
8. `git diff --check`
9. `pnpm tsgo:prod`
10. `pnpm check:test-types`
11. `pnpm check:architecture`
12. `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
13. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/vitest-scoped-config.test.ts`
14. `pnpm build:ci-artifacts`
15. `pnpm ui:build`

### Expected

- NEAR AI provider tests and plugin registry/provider-family contracts pass.
- Docs/plugin inventory checks pass.
- CLI lists `nearai/*` models.

### Actual

- All listed steps passed.
- Additional caveat: a broader bundled metadata test including `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts` currently fails on an unrelated `policy` startup-plugin expectation; this PR does not touch that plugin or expectation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: provider registration/onboarding, catalog build, public catalog mapping/filtering/fallback, docs/plugin inventory generation, and CLI provider model listing.
- Edge cases checked: retryable discovery failure falls back to the static catalog; non-chat utility rows are filtered; model compatibility flags use `max_tokens` and disable unsupported OpenAI-only fields.
- What you did **not** verify: live chat completion against NEAR AI Cloud because no `NEARAI_API_KEY` was available.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Users who want NEAR AI Cloud can set `NEARAI_API_KEY` or run `openclaw onboard --auth-choice nearai-api-key`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The bundled static fallback catalog may become stale as NEAR AI Cloud changes models.
  - Mitigation: The provider refreshes from NEAR AI Cloud's public model list at runtime and falls back to the bundled catalog only when discovery is unavailable.
- Risk: Live inference behavior was not exercised in this environment.
  - Mitigation: The implementation uses OpenClaw's existing OpenAI-compatible provider transport and includes unit coverage for request-shape compatibility metadata.

target.title: fix: avoid serializing plaintext model api keys
target.body:
## Summary
- Prevent config/source-managed plaintext provider API keys from being serialized into generated models.json contents.
- Preserve the models.json auth/registry contract by writing a non-secret `openclaw-managed` apiKey marker instead of deleting the field.
- Keep existing models.json-only plaintext keys when no source config owns the credential, avoiding upgrade-time loss of the user's only auth source.
- Add regression coverage for plaintext config values, managed env secret markers, existing models.json merges, the new marker, and pi-coding-agent registry acceptance.

Refs #11829.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: After the fix, a source-configured custom provider with a plaintext API key generates `models.json` without that plaintext key, while still keeping a non-empty `apiKey` marker so pi-coding-agent accepts the custom provider and model.

Real environment tested: Local Windows development checkout, commit `dd10217e`; temporary real OpenClaw agentDir under `C:\Users\Lenovo\AppData\Local\Temp`; actual `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps`, `discoverAuthStorage`, and `discoverModels` were executed via `node --import tsx --input-type=module`. The credential value was a fake redacted proof key, not a real provider secret.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created a temporary agentDir.
2. Planned `models.json` for a custom `openai-completions` provider whose source config contained fake plaintext `apiKey: sk-live-proof-redacted`.
3. Wrote the planned contents to the temporary agentDir's `models.json`.
4. Loaded that file through pi-coding-agent registry integration using `discoverAuthStorage` and `discoverModels`.

target.title: fix(media): normalize Windows inbound paths case-insensitively
target.body:
Related: #96024

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Windows users could have a valid inbound media file rejected when the configured media root and the reported file path use different casing for the drive path.

For example, a root configured as:

```text
C:\Users\Alice\Library\Messages\Attachments
```

could fail to match an actual file path reported as:

```text
c:\users\alice\Library\Messages\Attachments\12\34\ABCDEF\IMG_0001.jpeg
```

Windows drive paths are normally case-insensitive, but `packages/media-core/src/inbound-path-policy.ts` normalizes separators and then compares root and candidate path segments strictly. That made the inbound media policy treat the same Windows path as outside the allowed root.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps the existing POSIX behavior unchanged while normalizing only absolute Windows drive paths to a shared casing before segment comparison.

This keeps the change narrow: it only affects paths matching the Windows drive absolute-path form, such as `C:/Users/Alice/Media`. POSIX paths like `/Users/Alice/Media` remain case-sensitive.

The affected call chain is:

```text
local media / media-understanding inbound file path
-> media root allow-list check
-> isInboundPathAllowed(...)
-> normalizePosixAbsolutePath(...)
-> strict segment comparison
```

This is the shared policy used by inbound media path validation, so fixing the normalization point avoids caller-specific Windows workarounds.

## User Impact

Windows users can use local media roots even when the configured root and the file path reported by the runtime differ only by casing.

This prevents valid local media files from being rejected as outside the allowed inbound root. Linux and macOS-style POSIX path matching is unchanged.

## Evidence

Focused regression coverage was added for a mixed-case Windows drive root and candidate path:

```ts
isInboundPathAllowed({
  filePath: "C:\\Users\\Alice\\Library\\Messages\\Attachments\\12\\34\\ABCDEF\\IMG_0001.jpeg",
  roots: ["c:/users/*/library/messages/attachments"],
});
```

Before this fix, that path pair could be rejected because the segment comparison saw `C:` and `c:` as different strings. After this fix, the focused regression test confirms it is accepted.

Windows PowerShell behavior proof from this branch:

```powershell
$dir = Join-Path $env:TEMP "OpenClawCaseProof-$PID"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $dir | Out-Null
New-Item -ItemType File -Path (Join-Path $dir 'MixedCase.TXT') | Out-Null
$probe = Join-Path $dir 'mixedcase.txt'
[pscustomobject]@{
  Created = Join-Path $dir 'MixedCase.TXT'
  Probe = $probe
  TestPath = Test-Path -LiteralPath $probe
  GetItemName = (Get-Item -LiteralPath $probe).Name
} | Format-List
Remove-Item -LiteralPath $dir -Recurse -Force
```

```text
Created     : C:\Users\ZXY\AppData\Local\Temp\OpenClawCaseProof-12264\MixedCase.TXT
Probe       : C:\Users\ZXY\AppData\Local\Temp\OpenClawCaseProof-12264\mixedcase.txt
TestPath    : True
GetItemName : mixedcase.txt
```

Validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/media-core/src/inbound-path-policy.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       8 passed (8)
```

AI assistance was used to prepare this PR.

target.title: fix(deepseek): recover V4 zero-cost spend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97047.

DeepSeek V4 usage can still surface as zero spend when session transcripts contain nonzero token usage with `usage.cost.total: 0` even though the model has known positive pricing. That leaves Control UI Spend and session log views reporting known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage as zero.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change keeps the generic session-cost guard from preserving recorded zero totals for known-priced nonzero-token usage, clears stale cost breakdowns so aggregation uses the recomputed estimate, and bumps the usage-cost cache version so older cached zero totals are rebuilt.

It also keeps DeepSeek provider policy compatibility-safe: missing DeepSeek model `cost` metadata hydrates from the bundled catalog, but explicit all-zero `cost` overrides are preserved rather than silently replaced with catalog pricing.

## User Impact

DeepSeek V4 spend summaries, cache rebuilds, and session logs now show estimated cost for known-priced token usage instead of a fabricated zero.

Configured DeepSeek models without a `cost` block still recover bundled catalog pricing. Configured DeepSeek models with an explicit all-zero `cost` block remain zero-cost overrides.

## Review Items Addressed

- Fixed the `check:test-types` issue by using the cache summary API's typed `startMs`/`endMs` parameters.
- Fixed the session-log fixture so the transcript produces a returned log entry.
- Removed the direct `CHANGELOG.md` edit; release-note context now stays in this PR body only.
- Added redacted after-fix behavior proof for the known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded-zero-cost path.
- Addressed the ClawSweeper P1 compatibility finding by preserving explicit all-zero DeepSeek cost overrides and narrowing the provider-policy behavior to missing-cost hydration.

## Redacted behavior probe

Temporary state-dir probe with redacted transcript content and DeepSeek V4 catalog pricing:

```json
{
  "probe": "redacted DeepSeek V4 recorded-zero cost recompute",
  "inputTokens": 10000,
  "outputTokens": 10000,
  "cacheReadTokens": 10000,
  "recordedCostTotal": 0,
  "catalogCostPerMillion": {
    "input": 0.14,
    "output": 0.28,
    "cacheRead": 0.028,
    "cacheWrite": 0
  },
  "totalTokens": 30000,
  "totalCost": 0.00448,
  "sessionLog": {
    "tokens": 30000,
    "cost": 0.00448
  }
}
```

sessionLog cost 0.00448.

## Current head key Actions

- Recompute recorded-zero session transcript costs when the model has positive configured/catalog pricing and nonzero tokens.
- Clear stale zero breakdowns so spend summary and session log cost both use the recomputed estimate.
- Bump the usage-cost cache semantic version from 4 to 5 so old zero-preserving cache entries are rebuilt.
- Hydrate missing DeepSeek configured costs from bundled catalog pricing.
- Preserve explicit all-zero DeepSeek configured cost overrides.
- Keep `CHANGELOG.md` unchanged relative to `upstream/main`.

## Evidence

- `node --import tsx` redacted behavior probe: `"totalCost": 0.00448`; sessionLog cost 0.00448.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` passed locally on the PR branch.
- `git diff --check` passed locally on the PR branch.
- `CHANGELOG.md` has no diff relative to `upstream/main`.
- Added fixture coverage in `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` for known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded zero cost, including summary totals, cache upgrade behavior, and `loadSessionLogs()` cost display.
- Added fixture coverage in `extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` for missing-cost catalog hydration and explicit all-zero cost override preservation.

## Actions status

Current head `dd8cd0f`:

- Real behavior proof: success.
- `check-test-types`: success.
- QA Smoke CI: success.
- `check-docs`: skipped.
- `build-artifacts`: success.
- `check-lint`: success.
- `check-prod-types`: success.
- `check-shrinkwrap`: success.
- `check-dependencies`: success.
- `check-guards`: success.

target.title: fix(plugins): preserve bundled capability manifest contracts
target.body:
## Background
- bundled capability runtime rebuilt plugin records without carrying `manifest.contracts` forward
- that drops `contracts.tools` (and other declared contract metadata) for bundled capability plugins like Feishu/Lark
- downstream contract-based tooling sees the plugin as if it declared no tool contracts

## Changes
- pass `manifest.contracts` into `createCapabilityPluginRecord(...)`
- persist the contracts on the synthesized bundled capability plugin record
- initialize contract-backed provider id lists from the manifest data, matching normal plugin record creation
- add a regression test that verifies bundled capability runtime keeps manifest contracts for `memory-core`

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.ts src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.ts src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`

## Impact
- restores bundled capability manifest contract metadata for Feishu/Lark and other bundled capability plugins
- keeps contract-driven tool/provider resolution aligned with the main plugin loader path

Fixes #77982

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: bundled capability runtime preserves `manifest.contracts` on synthesized bundled capability plugin records instead of dropping them during registry loading.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the real bundled-capability runtime loader path, using the PR branch code against the repository's actual plugin manifests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused runtime/registry proof on the PR branch by loading the bundled capability registry and printing the synthesized `memory-core` / `feishu` records after runtime initialization.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts
✓ unit-fast ../../src/plugins/bundled-capability-runtime.test.ts (2 tests)
  ✓ preserves manifest contracts for bundled capability plugins

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       2 passed (2)
```

```text
$ pnpm exec tsx -e 'loadBundledCapabilityRuntimeRegistry(...)'
{
  "memoryCore": {
    "contracts": {
      "memoryEmbeddingProviders": ["local"],
      "tools": ["memory_get", "memory_search"]
    },
    "memoryEmbeddingProviderIds": ["local", "local"]
  },
  "feishu": {
    "contracts": {
      "tools": [
        "feishu_app_scopes",
        "feishu_bitable_create_app",
        "feishu_bitable_create_field",
        "feishu_bitable_create_record",
        "feishu_bitable_get_meta",
        "feishu_bitable_get_record",
        "feishu_bitable_list_fields",
        "feishu_bitable_list_records",
        "feishu_bitable_update_record",
        "feishu_chat",
        "feishu_doc",
        "feishu_drive",
        "feishu_perm",
        "feishu_wiki"
      ]
    },
    "toolNames": []
  }
}
```
- Observed result after fix: the synthesized bundled capability records keep `manifest.contracts` for both `memory-core` and `feishu` after registry loading, which is the runtime behavior this bug was dropping before the patch.
- What was not tested: no live Feishu/Lark message send flow; this proof covers the real bundled-capability runtime load path and emitted runtime records rather than downstream API traffic.
